perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will hold the Confidential Information in confidence and will not disclose the Confidential Information to any person or entity, except close family members, without the prior written consent of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except close family members' introduces a conflict with the general prohibition of disclosure stated in the original text. While this exception is now defined here, section III regarding unauthorized disclosure does not account for this exception, creating an ambiguity of whether disclosure to family members would trigger an injunction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION - INJUNCTION. If it appears that the Recipient has disclosed (or has threatened to disclose) Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement, the Owner shall be entitled to an injunction to restrain the Recipient from disclosing the Confidential Information in whole or in part. The Owner shall not be prohibited by this provision from pursuing other remedies, including a claim for losses and damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited disclosure of confidential information to any person or entity without the owner's consent. The changed text creates an exception for 'close family members.' This directly contradicts the 'UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION - INJUNCTION' section, which states that any disclosure (or threatened disclosure) entitles the Owner to an injunction. The changed text's exception is not accounted for in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction by introducing an ambiguity as to whether disclosing to family members is an unauthorized disclosure that would trigger an injunction. The 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition is met because the modified text introduces an inconsistent obligation regarding what constitutes an unauthorized disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Recipient shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the written request of the Owner, the Recipient shall return to the Owner all written materials containing the Confidential Information, unless the Recipient deems it necessary to retain a copy for personal records. The Recipient shall also deliver to the Owner written statements signed by the Recipient certifying that all materials have been returned within five (5) days of receipt of the request.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'unless the Recipient deems it necessary to retain a copy for personal records,' it contradicts the original intent of ensuring all confidential information is returned, creating an ambiguity on whether the recipient is fully returning what is confidential information. This undermines the overall confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GENERAL PROVISIONS. This Agreement sets forth the entire unde rstanding of the parties regarding confidentiality. The obligations of confidentiality shall survive indefinitely from the date of disclosure of the Proprietary Information. Any amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties. This Agreement shall be construed under the laws of the State of Florida. This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party. Neither party may delegate its duties under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. The confidentiality provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect at all times after the effective date of this Agreement. If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the remaining portions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect and construed so as to best effectuate the original inten t and purpose of this Agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause ('unless the Recipient deems it necessary to retain a copy for personal records') that allows the Recipient to keep confidential information, directly contradicting the contradicted text's declaration that the agreement represents the 'entire understanding of the parties regarding confidentiality' and that the 'confidentiality provisions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect at all times'. The changed text creates an ambiguity regarding whether all confidential information is returned, undermining the confidentiality agreement's intention, therefore there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information is provided on an "AS IS" basis. THE OWNER MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WITH RESPECT TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION AND HEREBY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE OWNER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH OR ARISING OUT OF THE PERFORMANCE OR USE OF ANY PORTION OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information is provided on an "AS IS" basis. THE OWNER MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WITH RESPECT TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION EXCEPT THAT THE OWNER WARRANTS THAT TO THE BEST OF THEIR KNOWLEDGE IT DOES NOT INFRINGE ON ANY THIRD-PARTY INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE OWNER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH OR ARISING OUT OF THE PERFORMANCE OR USE OF ANY PORTION OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: By adding a limited warranty concerning non-infringement of intellectual property rights, the modified section introduces an element of warranty where previously there was none. This directly contradicts the initial disclaimer of *any and all* implied warranties, potentially opening the door for legal challenges if the information does, in fact, infringe on third-party rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NO WARRANTY. The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information is provided on an "AS IS" basis. THE OWNER MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, WITH RESPECT TO THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION EXCEPT THAT THE OWNER WARRANTS THAT TO THE BEST OF THEIR KNOWLEDGE IT DOES NOT INFRINGE ON ANY THIRD-PARTY INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE OWNER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES IN CONNECTION WITH OR ARISING OUT OF THE PERFORMANCE OR USE OF ANY PORTION OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The Owner does not represe nt or warrant that any product or business plans disclosed to the Recipient will be marketed or carried out as disclosed, or at all. Any actions taken by the Recipient in response to the disclosure of the Confidential Information shall be solely at the risk of the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims 'any and all implied warranties,' while the changed text introduces a specific warranty regarding non-infringement of intellectual property rights. This directly contradicts the earlier disclaimer, creating a strong inconsistency within the document, as the Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text. The explanation given also supports this contradiction.
